questions: Item 3: Should we extract a shared hook (usePortalMousedownGuard) to deduplicate the workaround, or just standardize the comment and leave the inline useEffects? The hook saves ~10 lines per site but adds a new file.

Item 4: The current optimistic pattern provides instant UI feedback when adding images. Switching to invalidation-only means a brief delay before the image appears in the timeline. Is that acceptable, or should we keep some form of optimistic update (e.g., use the DB response to directly setQueryData instead of just invalidating)?
Item 1: Should the new SQL migration use CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION (in-place update) or DROP + CREATE? CREATE OR REPLACE is simpler but requires the same return type.

success_criteria.criterion: duplicate_as_new_generation RPC no longer accepts p_target_timeline_frame parameter
No console.log with [ShotCreate] tags remain in the codebase
All three portal stopPropagation workarounds have clear documentation of the root cause
addImageToShot mutation no longer generates temp IDs or uses replaceOptimisticItemInCache
applyOptimisticCaches, replaceOptimisticItemInCache, replaceOptimisticItemInShotsCache are removed from addImageToShotHelpers.ts
npx tsc --noEmit passes with no errors
All existing tests in src/shared/hooks/shots/ pass (updated as needed)
Portal workaround is extracted into a shared hook to reduce duplication
addImageToShot onSuccess uses direct cache update (setQueryData) rather than invalidation-only, to preserve instant UI feedback
SQL migration uses CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION for clean upgrade path
Manual test: duplicate image in shot editor places correctly without target_timeline_frame
Manual test: add image to shot appears in timeline without temp-ID flicker

assumptions: No caller actually passes target_timeline_frame — verified by searching src/ directory. The field is dead code.
The three [ShotCreate] logs are only in TimelineEditor.tsx (verified — shotCreationPaths.ts has none).
The portal Node.contains() issue is fundamental to createPortal + native DOM event listeners, not a React 18 bug. The stopPropagation workaround is the correct fix.

For item 4, the brief mentions switching to 'the same atomic pattern' — I'm interpreting this as invalidation-based (like useDuplicateAsNewGeneration), but a hybrid approach using setQueryData with the DB response would preserve instant feedback. The plan includes a question about this.
skipOptimistic is only used internally within useShotGenerationMutations and possibly by direct callers — will search and update all sites.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION is safe here since the return type (jsonb) is unchanged — only the parameter list changes, which CREATE OR REPLACE supports.
